LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE COURT OR THE OPPOSING PARTY TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CARPENTERS 46 NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COUNTIES CONFERENCE BOARD'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SERVE A PROPER SUMMONS AND THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION IS NON-RESPONSIVE AND IT IS PLAINTIFF'S BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE JURISDICTION/PROPER SERVICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT GRANTED A CONTINUANCE OF PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' NOTICE OF MOTION TO APPLY SANCTIONS AND PENALTIES ON DEFENDANTS TO FEBRUARY 19, 2014 AT 9:00 AM. MS. WILLIAMS WAS ORDERED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 7, 2014. JUDGE PRO TEM: JEFFREY WOHL, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE COURT AND THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE JUDGMENT, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT NOTWITHSTANDING THE VERDICT UNDER CCP 663A AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH TITLED A "MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE JUDGMENT, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT NOTWITHSTANDING THE VERDICT UNDER CCP 663A, MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION," PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS ESSENTIALLY MOVES FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER ON MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT ISSUED ON FEBRUARY 20, 2014. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT THERE ARE ANY NEW FACTS OR LAW EXIST TO WARRANT RECONSIDERATION PURSUANT TO CCP 1008. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS RELIEF FROM JUDGMENT, THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS PREMATURE; NO JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' MOTION TO APPLY SANCTIONS AND PENALTIES ON DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION LACKS A STATUTORY BASIS. FURTHER, THE COURT ALREADY DETERMINED BY ORDER ON MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT (FILED FEBRUARY 20, 2014) THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SERVE A PROPER SUMMONS AND THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT ON DEFENDANT CARPENTERS 46 NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COUNTIES CONFERENCE BOARD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Off Calendar Jun-02-2014 Continued To Aug-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Lori Williams Motion To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defendants
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' Motion To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defendants is denied. Plaintiff's motion lacks a statutory basis. Further, the Court already determined by Order on Motion to Quash Service of Summons and Complaint (filed February 20, 2014) that plaintiff failed to serve a proper summons and the operative complaint on Defendant Carpenters 46 Northern California Counties Conference Board. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Ntc Of Mtn To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defs
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' Motion To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defendants is transferred to be heard on the law and motion calendar in room 302 at 9:30 a.m. on the same date. JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgemtn, Motion For Judgment Notwithstanding The Verdict Under Ccp 663a. Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Apr-14-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - LORI WILLIAMS, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE COURT AND THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment, Motion For Judgment Notwithstanding The Verdict Under Ccp 663a and Motion For Reconsideration is Denied. Although titled a "Motion to Set Aside and Vacate Judgment, Motion for Judgment Notwithstanding the Verdict Under CCP 663a, Motion for Reconsideration," Plaintiff Lori Williams essentially moves for reconsideration of the Order on Motion to Quash Service of Summons and Complaint issued on February 20, 2014. Plaintiff has not demonstrate that there are any new facts or law exist to warrant reconsideration pursuant to CCP 1008. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks relief from judgment, the requested relief is premature; no judgment has been entered. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Mtn To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defs
Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Continued To Feb-19-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lori Williams, self-represented litigant, appearing;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Matter called. Ms. Williams requested a continuance to March 12 noting that counsel for the other side did agree to the continuance. The Court granted a continuance of PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' Notice Of Motion To Apply Sanctions And Penalties On Defendants to February 19, 2014 at 9:00 am. Ms. Williams was ordered to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302 by the close of business on Friday, February 7, 2014. Judge Pro Tem: Jeffrey Wohl, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - LORI WILLIAMS, TEL. NO. (650) 544-4851, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER

RULING - NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE COURT OR THE OPPOSING PARTY TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CARPENTERS 46 NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COUNTIES CONFERENCE BOARD'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is granted. Defendant demonstrates that plaintiff failed to serve a proper summons and the operative complaint. Plaintiff's opposition is non-responsive and it is plaintiff's burden to demonstrate jurisdiction/proper service. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 27, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied.
